---
title: "Multivariat TK3"
author: "Faiz Iqbal I'tishom"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
---

```{r}
# Load the libraries
library(lavaan)
library(semPlot)
library(psych)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)

# Load the data (assuming data_3 is your dataset)
data_3 <- read_csv("C:/Uner/Semester 5/Multivariat/Coolyeah/Tugas Kelompok 3/Wellbeing_and_lifestyle_data_Kaggle.csv")
```

```{r}
# Periksa nama kolom dalam dataset
colnames(data_3)

```

```{r}

# Mengonversi variabel DAILY_STRESS ke numerik
data_3$DAILY_STRESS <- as.numeric(data_3$DAILY_STRESS)

data_3 <- na.omit(data_3)

# Cek hasil konversi
str(data_3$DAILY_STRESS)

# Periksa struktur dataset
str(data_3)

```


```{r}
# Step 1: Select only numeric columns for analysis
numeric_data <- data_3[, sapply(data_3, is.numeric)]

# Step 2: Compute correlation matrix only with relevant numeric columns
cor_matrix <- cor(numeric_data[, c("FRUITS_VEGGIES", "DAILY_STEPS", "BMI_RANGE", "SLEEP_HOURS", "LOST_VACATION", 
                                    "CORE_CIRCLE", "SUPPORTING_OTHERS", "SOCIAL_NETWORK", "PLACES_VISITED", "DONATION", 
                                    "DAILY_STRESS", "FLOW", "LIVE_VISION", "TIME_FOR_PASSION", "WEEKLY_MEDITATION", 
                                    "WORK_LIFE_BALANCE_SCORE", "ACHIEVEMENT", "TODO_COMPLETED", "SUFFICIENT_INCOME", 
                                    "PERSONAL_AWARDS")])

# Step 3: Run EFA to determine the number of factors
library(psych)
efa_result <- fa(cor_matrix, nfactors = 5, rotate = "varimax", fm = "minres")
print(efa_result)

# Step 4: Visualize the factor loadings
fa.diagram(efa_result)
```

```{r}
# Define the CFA model
cfa_model <- '
  # Measurement model
    # Kesehatan Fisik (ξ₁)
    Kesehatan_Fisik =~ FRUITS_VEGGIES + DAILY_STEPS + BMI_RANGE + SLEEP_HOURS + LOST_VACATION
    # Relasi Sosial (ξ₂)
    Relasi_Sosial =~ CORE_CIRCLE + SUPPORTING_OTHERS + SOCIAL_NETWORK + PLACES_VISITED + DONATION
    # Kesejahteraan Psikologis (ξ₃)
    Kesejahteraan_Psikologis =~ DAILY_STRESS + FLOW + LIVE_VISION + TIME_FOR_PASSION + WEEKLY_MEDITATION
    # Kesejahteraan Umum (η₁)
    Kesejahteraan_Umum =~ WORK_LIFE_BALANCE_SCORE + ACHIEVEMENT + TODO_COMPLETED + SUFFICIENT_INCOME + PERSONAL_AWARDS
'

# Fit the CFA model
fit_cfa <- cfa(cfa_model, data = numeric_data)

# Step 2: Evaluate goodness of fit
summary(fit_cfa, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)

# Step 3: Visualize the CFA model using semPlot
library(semPlot)
semPaths(fit_cfa, whatLabels = "std", layout = "spring", edge.label.cex = 1.2, sizeMan = 10, sizeLat = 12)


```


```{r}
# Define the SEM model
sem_model <- '
  # Measurement model
    # Kesehatan Fisik (ξ₁)
    Kesehatan_Fisik =~ FRUITS_VEGGIES + DAILY_STEPS + BMI_RANGE + SLEEP_HOURS + LOST_VACATION
    # Relasi Sosial (ξ₂)
    Relasi_Sosial =~ CORE_CIRCLE + SUPPORTING_OTHERS + SOCIAL_NETWORK + PLACES_VISITED + DONATION
    # Kesejahteraan Psikologis (ξ₃)
    Kesejahteraan_Psikologis =~ DAILY_STRESS + FLOW + LIVE_VISION + TIME_FOR_PASSION + WEEKLY_MEDITATION
    # Kesejahteraan Umum (η₁)
    Kesejahteraan_Umum =~ WORK_LIFE_BALANCE_SCORE + ACHIEVEMENT + TODO_COMPLETED + SUFFICIENT_INCOME + PERSONAL_AWARDS

  # Structural model
    Kesejahteraan_Umum ~ Kesehatan_Fisik + Relasi_Sosial + Kesejahteraan_Psikologis
    Kesejahteraan_Psikologis ~ Relasi_Sosial + Kesehatan_Fisik
    Kesehatan_Fisik ~ Relasi_Sosial
'

# Fit the SEM model
fit_sem <- sem(sem_model, data = numeric_data)

# Step 2: Evaluate goodness of fit
summary(fit_sem, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)

# Step 3: Visualize the SEM model
semPaths(fit_sem, whatLabels = "std", layout = "spring", edge.label.cex = 1.2, sizeMan = 10, sizeLat = 12)

```

```{r}
# Cek varians dari setiap variabel
apply(numeric_data, 2, var)
```
```{r}
# Matriks korelasi antar variabel
cor(numeric_data)
```


```{r}
# Fit the CFA model
fit_cfa <- cfa(cfa_model, data = numeric_data)

# Step 2: Display the summary of the CFA model with fit measures and standardized estimates
summary(fit_cfa, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE)

```


```{r}
# Define the SEM model
sem_model <- '
  # Measurement model
    # Kesehatan Fisik (ξ₁)
    Kesehatan_Fisik =~ FRUITS_VEGGIES + DAILY_STEPS + BMI_RANGE + SLEEP_HOURS + LOST_VACATION
    # Relasi Sosial (ξ₂)
    Relasi_Sosial =~ CORE_CIRCLE + SUPPORTING_OTHERS + SOCIAL_NETWORK + PLACES_VISITED + DONATION
    # Kesejahteraan Psikologis (ξ₃)
    Kesejahteraan_Psikologis =~ DAILY_STRESS + FLOW + LIVE_VISION + TIME_FOR_PASSION + WEEKLY_MEDITATION
    # Kesejahteraan Umum (η₁)
    Kesejahteraan_Umum =~ WORK_LIFE_BALANCE_SCORE + ACHIEVEMENT + TODO_COMPLETED + SUFFICIENT_INCOME + PERSONAL_AWARDS

  # Structural model
    Kesejahteraan_Umum ~ Kesehatan_Fisik + Relasi_Sosial + Kesejahteraan_Psikologis
    Kesejahteraan_Psikologis ~ Relasi_Sosial + Kesehatan_Fisik
    Kesehatan_Fisik ~ Relasi_Sosial
'

# Fit the SEM model
fit_sem <- sem(sem_model, data = numeric_data)

# Step 2: Display the summary of the SEM model with fit measures, standardized estimates, and R-squared
summary(fit_sem, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)

```

```{r}
# Cek matriks korelasi antar variabel
cor(numeric_data[, c("FRUITS_VEGGIES", "DAILY_STEPS", "BMI_RANGE", "SLEEP_HOURS", "LOST_VACATION", 
                     "CORE_CIRCLE", "SUPPORTING_OTHERS", "SOCIAL_NETWORK", "PLACES_VISITED", "DONATION", 
                     "DAILY_STRESS", "FLOW", "LIVE_VISION", "TIME_FOR_PASSION", "WEEKLY_MEDITATION", 
                     "WORK_LIFE_BALANCE_SCORE", "ACHIEVEMENT", "TODO_COMPLETED", "SUFFICIENT_INCOME", 
                     "PERSONAL_AWARDS")])

```

```{r}
# Standardisasi data
numeric_data_standardized <- scale(numeric_data)

# Fit SEM model dengan data yang sudah distandarisasi
fit_sem_standardized <- sem(sem_model, data = numeric_data_standardized)
summary(fit_sem_standardized, fit.measures = TRUE, standardized = TRUE, rsquare = TRUE)

```


```{r}
# Memeriksa matriks kovarians variabel laten
lavInspect(fit_sem, "cov.lv")

# Memeriksa pesan error lebih lanjut
lavInspect(fit_sem, "est")

```
